title: hieracium caesium subsp. resupinatum
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. resupinatum, also known as mountain hawkweed or resupinate hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Hieracium caesium subsp. resupinatum are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and exhibiting antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used as a tea or infused in oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
